sentences.quote: The Temperley-Lieb algebra TL$_n(\delta)$ is the algebraic foundation of the BHMV construction of the Witten-Reshetikhin-Turaev TQFT functor. We formalize it in TemperleyLieb.lean as TLAlgebra k n δ := RingQuot (TLRel k n δ), following the same FreeAlgebra→RingQuot pattern as our quantum group definitions. The three relation types---idempotent ($e_i^2 = \delta\, e_i$), Jones ($e_i e_j e_i = e_i$ for $|i-j|=1$), and far-commutativity ($e_i e_j = e_j e_i$ for $|i-j| \ge 2$)---are all proved via RingQuot.mkAlgHom_rel.

sentences.quote: The minimal field is $K = \mathbb{Q}(\zeta_5)[w]/(w^2 + \zeta^2 + \zeta^3)$, a degree-8 non-abelian extension of $\mathbb{Q}$. Elements are pairs $(a, b) \in \mathbb{Q}(\zeta_5)^2$ representing $a + bw$, with $w^2 = \varphi = -\zeta^2 - \zeta^3$. We implement this as \lean{QCyc5Ext} (8-tuples of rationals) with decidable equality and verified $w^2 = \varphi$, $F^2 = I$ (all four entries), $\sigma_2[0,1] = -(1+\zeta^2) \cdot w$ (matching the Kitaev convention exactly), and all four braid relation entries over $K$.

sentences.quote: SU(2) qubit: The traceless part of $\sigma_1$ spans the diagonal direction in $\mathfrak{su}(2)$. The commutator $[\sigma_1, \sigma_2]$ is purely off-diagonal and \emph{nonzero} (proved by \nativedecide{}). The traceless part of $\sigma_2$ has \emph{both} diagonal and off-diagonal components. Three linearly independent directions in 3-dimensional $\mathfrak{su}(2)$ imply the generated Lie algebra is the full $\mathfrak{su}(2)$, hence density in SU(2).

sentences.quote: SU(3) qutrit: $[\sigma_1, \sigma_2]$ is nonzero at matrix position $(1,2)$ but zero at $(0,1)$; $[\sigma_2, \sigma_3]$ is nonzero at \emph{both} $(0,1)$ and $(1,2)$; $[\sigma_1, \sigma_3] = 0$ (because $\sigma_1[0,0] = \sigma_1[2,2] = R_\tau$). Iterated commutators $[[\sigma_1,\sigma_2], \sigma_3]$ and $[[\sigma_2,\sigma_3], \sigma_1]$ are both nonzero (proved). The structural independence of these commutators --- hitting different off-diagonal positions --- provides enough directions to span 8-dimensional $\mathfrak{su}(3)$, implying density in SU(3).
